questions.question: A recipe for making bread requires $3^2$ cups of flour. If you want to make $2^2$ times the amount of bread, how many cups of flour will you need?
A building is $2^3$ meters tall. If a new building is $2^2$ times taller, how tall is the new building?
A chef needs to package $3^2$ boxes of cookies. If each box can hold $2^3$ cookies, how many cookies can be packaged in total?
A carpenter needs to make $2^3$ sets of stairs. If each set requires $3^2$ pieces of wood, how many pieces of wood will the carpenter need in total?

A bakery is making a cake that requires $2^3$ cups of sugar. If they want to make $2^2$ times the amount of cake, and each cup of sugar costs $3^2$ dollars, how much will the sugar cost in total? Show your work and simplify your answer.

A construction company is building a skyscraper that is $2^4$ meters tall. If they want to add $2^3$ more meters to the height, and each meter of the building requires $3^2$ kilograms of steel, how many kilograms of steel will the company need in total? Show your work and simplify your answer.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to use exponent rules to simplify expressions.
Tip 2: Remind students to consider the context of the problem when applying exponent rules.
Tip 3: Emphasize the importance of showing work and simplifying answers in free-response questions.
